Quasar Markets Newsletter TL;DR

Japan's prime minister reelection and HSBC's upgrade of L’Oréal highlight global market shifts, while JPMorgan favors international stocks over American ones.

Market Movers:
NVIDIA ($NVDA), Ondas Holdings ($ONDS), Tesla ($TSLA) show varied activity; IBRX gains on Saudi approval, ONDS rebounds with German police deal.

Corporate Actions:
Peter Thiel's Founders Fund exits ETHZilla; MoonLake stock surges on takeover speculation.

Economic Indicators:
No significant updates.

Market Sentiment:
European stocks poised for gains; JPMorgan sees potential in international equities, contrasting with American market outlook. Quasar Markets Newsletter TL;DR

Building features isn’t bad. Building them for the wrong reason is.

New features make sense when they’re pulled out of real feedback. When users say, “I wish it could do this,” and it fits where the product is headed, that’s a green light.

The trap is using features as a crutch when growth stalls.

If marketing isn’t working and nobody’s using the product, adding more surface area won’t fix it. It just delays, and sometimes complicates, the real work.

The only way to know what to build next is to ship something raw, get it into the hands of people outside your bubble, and listen carefully.

Not to every random Joe. But to genuine user feedback.

That feedback loop makes or breaks a product. Building features isn’t bad. Building them for the wrong reason is.

The only way to know what to build next is to ship something raw, get it into the hands of people outside your bubble, and listen carefully.

Not to every random Joe. But to genuine user feedback.

That feedback loop makes or breaks a product.

We work hard.

We take this seriously too.

On today’s MarketMinds, I’m breaking down put/call parity—the foundational pricing relationship that keeps options markets aligned—and why it matters more than most traders realize.

Here’s what’s on tap:
-What put/call parity actually tells you about fair value in today’s market
-When arbitrage setups appear, and why they disappear fast
-How this principle helps you structure smarter spreads and hedges

Monthly reminder that if you sell health insurance, your ability to sell someone on the phone could save their life.

And if you make a career out of selling health insurance, there’s a near 100% probability that some of the phone calls you’ve taken have saved lives indirectly by providing them with insurance they otherwise wouldn’t have purchased.

For this reason, it’s your ethical responsibility to become as exceptional a salesperson as possible. Lives are quite literally at stake.

If that doesn’t motivate you, I don’t know what will. Monthly reminder that if you sell health insurance, your ability to sell someone on the phone could save their life.

Which paid ads platform works best for insurance leads?

That depends…

Google Search Ads are the highest intent.

People are literally typing in “Best life insurance in 2026.”

But high intent comes at a price.

Cost per call can run from $50 to $150, depending on your market and competition.

Whereas Facebook and Instagram ads are lower intent.

Cost per call could be $15-$40, depending on the industry.

The key is testing. Because you never really know until you try.

I know for a fact they all work, but which is the best bang for your buck? That depends.

So either test and see what happens, or buy high-intent inbound leads from someone else instead. Which paid ads platform works best for insurance leads?

What’s one of the biggest reasons solo agents join a team?

For access to quality leads, of course.

So if you aspire to build a killer sales team to sell insurance, it behooves you to learn how to generate quality leads first.

Or at the very least, sign up with a platform that has access to them.

That’s why at CallSync, our platform operates like an insurance agency in a box.

You can whitelabel it to your brand, so your team can plug in, start taking inbound calls under your company name, and scale without worrying about lead flow.

Boom. Lead quality problem solved.

You can leverage our platform while you learn to generate quality leads yourself, or just keep kickin' it with us.

a recruitment agency owner told me he trains reps for 6 months. they get good. they leave.

rinse and repeat. forever.

I asked: "what if the training never walked out the door?"

he went quiet for 10 seconds.

that silence? that's the moment someone realizes the old way is broken.

I talked to 50+ restaurant owners trying to sell my POS system.

Every single one said no.

Not because the product was bad - 8 million lines of code, AI-powered, beautiful UI.

Because I had no sales team and no distribution strategy.

Building something incredible means nothing if nobody knows it exists.

That failure taught me more about business than my CS degree ever did.

Now I build sales systems instead of products. The irony writes itself.

Day 37 of building in public #BuildInPublic I talked to 50+ restaurant owners trying to sell my POS system.

Every single one said no.

Not because the product was bad - 8 million lines of code, AI-powered, beautiful UI.

Because I had no sales team and no distribution strategy.

Building something incredible means nothing if nobody knows it exists.

That failure taught me more about business than my CS degree ever did.

Now I build sales systems instead of products. The irony writes itself.

Day 37 of building in public #BuildInPublic

Unpopular opinion: your AI SDR tool is just an expensive email blaster.

Here is why most fail:

- They send the same template to 10K people
- They book meetings with unqualified leads
- They have zero memory of past conversations
- They treat every prospect like a stranger

A real AI sales system learns what works for YOUR specific business after 50 conversations.

It remembers every objection, every close, every loss.

That is the difference between automation and intelligence. Unpopular opinion: your AI SDR tool is just an expensive email blaster.

PROMPT: "A hyper-realistic {{object}} rendered as a sculptural object carved entirely from solid, optical-grade crystal glass. The object preserves its true proportions and recognizable silhouette, with precise geometry and clean edge definition appropriate to its form. It is floating in space at a slight dynamic angle, not perfectly straight, allowing light interaction across multiple surfaces.

A single focused white light source enters the object from one direction, refracting through the internal volume and producing layered internal reflections, chromatic aberration, and physically accurate spectral dispersion along edges, corners, and internal transitions. Light bends, splits, and bounces naturally within the material, creating complex but controlled optical behavior.

Inside the glass, subtle volumetric depth is visible, including faint internal reflections, refractive distortions, micro-scratches, and tiny dust particles suspended within the material. A small, intense specular highlight appears on a sharp edge or curvature where the light strikes most directly, producing a crisp optical spark.

Reflections are sharp and physically accurate. The glass feels heavy, dense, and premium — never plastic, never stylized, never neon. The background is a pure black void with soft falloff and no visible environment. Lighting is minimal and studio-controlled, designed to emphasize form, refraction, internal depth, and edge clarity rather than brightness.

No text, no logos, no markings.",

    "lighting": "Low-key studio lighting with a single directional white light source",

    "background": "Pure black void",
    "mood": "Minimal, futuristic, scientific, museum-grade artifact",
    "color_behavior": "Rainbow dispersion only from white light refraction, never artificially colored",
    "Moodboard": "Attached Images"
  },

Aquí tienes 8 prompts brutales de Claude Opus 4.6 + Kling AI que crean videos virales de 10 minutos en horas.

R to @arceyul: PROMPT 8:

El Optimizador de Subidas y Algoritmo

Eres un Especialista en Algoritmos de YouTube.
Crea una estrategia de subida para este video:
Especificaciones del video:
• Duración: [X] minutos
• Tamaño del archivo: [X] GB
• Tiempo de procesamiento: [ESTIMADO]
OPTIMIZACIÓN DE SUBIDA:
1. METADATOS
• Título: [FINAL]
• Descripción (primeras 2 líneas visibles):
• Frase gancho (hook sentence).
• Marcas de tiempo / Capítulos (timestamps).
• Enlaces a recursos.
• Divulgación de afiliados (affiliate disclosures).
• Etiquetas (Tags): [5-8 PALABRAS CLAVE DE ALTA RELEVANCIA]
• Categoría: [LA MÁS ADECUADA]
• Idioma: [OBJETIVO]
• Subtítulos: [SUBIR ARCHIVO .SRT]
2. ELEMENTOS DE PANTALLA FINAL
• Botón de suscripción: [POSICIÓN]
• Video relacionado: [VIDEO ESPECÍFICO]
• Lista de reproducción: [LISTA RELEVANTE]
• Tiempo: [X:XX - 20 segundos antes del final]
3. TARJETAS (Máximo 2-3)
• Marca de tiempo 1: [X:XX] → Enlace a: [VIDEO]
• Marca de tiempo 2: [X:XX] → Enlace a: [LISTA DE REPRODUCCIÓN]
4. ESTRATEGIA DE COMENTARIO FIJADO
• Texto del comentario: [PREGUNTA / CTA / RECURSO]
• Dar "corazón" a todas las respuestas durante la primera hora.
5. PROGRAMACIÓN DE SUBIDA
• Mejor hora para [NICHO]: [DÍA / HORA]
• Consideración de zona horaria: [AUDIENCIA PRINCIPAL]
• Consistencia: [CALENDARIO / HORARIO]
6. CHECKLIST DE LAS PRIMERAS 24 HORAS
• [ ] Responder a los primeros 50 comentarios.
• [ ] Compartir en plataformas sociales.
• [ ] Notificación a la lista de correo.
• [ ] Publicación en la pestaña de Comunidad.
• [ ] Fijar en el canal (si es un video destacado).
7. SEÑALES DEL ALGORITMO A MONITOREAR
• CTR: Tasa de clics.
• AVD: Duración promedio de vista.
• Tasa de interacción (Engagement): Likes / comentarios / compartidos.
• Fuentes de tráfico: Navegación vs. Búsqueda vs. Sugeridos.
Crea una lista de verificación (checklist) completa de subida con prioridades de optimización. PROMPT 8:
